2605.20127.title: Beyond Prediction Accuracy: Target-Space Recovery Profiles for Evaluating Model-Brain Alignment
2605.20127.paper_summary: Artificial vision models are often evaluated against the human visual cortex by measuring how accurately their internal representations predict brain responses. However, prediction accuracy alone does not indicate which dimensions of the target brain's response space are recovered. Here, we introduce a unified framework for evaluating both model-brain and brain-brain alignment by identifying the response dimensions recovered by prediction. Using repeated fMRI measurements, we first identify target-brain response dimensions that can be reproducibly predicted across independent trial splits. We then predict target-brain responses from either another subject's brain responses or a vision model's internal representations, and quantify how strongly each of these reproducible response dimensions is recovered. Applying this framework to a subset of the Natural Scenes Dataset, in which eight subjects viewed the same natural images during fMRI, we find that the early-to-intermediate visual-cortex responses contain a low-dimensional set of reproducible dimensions. Brain-to-brain comparisons identify which of these dimensions are consistently recoverable from other subjects' brains, providing a diagnostic human reference rather than only a scalar benchmark. In some cases, pretrained and randomly initialized models achieve similar prediction accuracy while showing distinct recovery profiles across these response dimensions. These results show that prediction accuracy alone can mask model-brain mismatches. By making explicit which reproducible brain response dimensions are recovered by prediction, our framework provides a more diagnostic evaluation of alignment between artificial vision models and the human visual cortex.
2605.20127.paper_summary_zh: 人工視覺模型通常是通過測量其內部表徵預測大腦反應的準確性來評估與人類視覺皮層的對比。<lb/>然而，僅僅依賴預測準確性並不能指示目標大腦反應空間的哪些維度被恢復。<lb/>在這裡，我們介紹了一個統一的框架，用於通過識別預測所恢復的反應維度來評估模型與大腦之間以及大腦與大腦之間的對齊。<lb/>通過重複的 fMRI 測量，我們首先識別可以在獨立試驗分割中可重複預測的目標大腦反應維度。<lb/>然後，我們從另一個受試者的大腦反應或視覺模型的內部表徵中預測目標大腦反應，並量化每個可重複反應維度的恢復強度。<lb/>將這一框架應用於自然場景數據集的一個子集，在該子集中，八名受試者在 fMRI 中觀看相同的自然圖像，我們發現早期到中期的視覺皮層反應包含一組低維度的可重複維度。<lb/>大腦之間的比較識別出這些維度中哪些是可以從其他受試者的大腦中一致恢復的，提供了一個診斷性的人工參考，而不僅僅是一個標量基準。<lb/>在某些情況下，預訓練和隨機初始化的模型在預測準確性上達到相似的水平，同時在這些反應維度上顯示出不同的恢復特徵。<lb/>這些結果表明，僅僅依賴預測準確性可能會掩蓋模型與大腦之間的不匹配。<lb/>通過明確指出哪些可重複的大腦反應維度是通過預測恢復的，我們的框架提供了對人工視覺模型與人類視覺皮層之間對齊的更具診斷性的評估。
2605.20127.author: Ken Nakamura et.al.
2605.20127.authors: Ken Nakamura, Tomoya Nakai, Ryuto Yashiro, Ayumu Yamashita, Kaoru Amano
